title: Trump urges UK, China, France and others to send ships to secure Strait of Hormuz
subhead: The US president's call came after a string of US attacks on the key oil terminal Kharg Island on Friday.

title: Why is Kharg Island, Iran's key oil export hub, so important?
subhead: Tehran threatened to 'destroy and turn into a pile of ashes' US energy and oil infrastructure in the region after US strikes on the key island on Friday.

title: Zelensky and European leaders warn the US against easing sanctions on Russia
subhead: During a visit to Paris, the Ukrainian president criticized the partial lifting of sanctions on Russian oil. The measure, decided by the US administration in response to soaring prices caused by the war in Iran, strengthens Moscow's position in Ukraine.

title: Jürgen Habermas, influential German philosopher, dies at 96
subhead: The German philosopher died on Saturday March 14, 2026, at the age of 96 in Starnberg, a spokesperson for the Suhrkamp publishing house said.

title: How a billiard hall in Paris's Belleville neighborhood became a symbol of gentrification
subhead: Every evening, a new crowd of young creatives packs M8, a billiards hall with six tables located in Paris's 19th arrondissement. As those working in the luxury industry started coming, the venue's original regulars gradually faded away.

title: Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
subhead: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.
